moduleDescription:
This module implements a series of low level methods for bit manipulation.

By default, compiler intrinsics are used where possible to improve performance on supported compilers: GCC, LLVM_GCC, CLANG, VCC, ICC.

The module will fallback to pure nim procs in case the backend is not supported. You can also use the flag noIntrinsicsBitOpts to disable compiler intrinsics.

This module is also compatible with other backends: JavaScript, NimScript as well as the compiletime VM.

As a result of using optimized functions/intrinsics, some functions can return undefined results if the input is invalid. You can use the flag noUndefinedBitOpts to force predictable behaviour for all input, causing a small performance hit.

At this time only fastLog2, firstSetBit, countLeadingZeroBits and countTrailingZeroBits may return undefined and/or platform dependent values if given invalid input.

entries.name: bitnot
entries.code:
func bitnot[T: SomeInteger](x: T): T {.magic: "BitnotI", raises: [], tags: [],
                                       forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the bitwise complement of the integer x.

entries.name: bitand
entries.code: macro bitand[T: SomeInteger](x, y: T; z: varargs[T]): T
entries.description: Computes the bitwise and of all arguments collectively.

entries.name: bitor
entries.code: macro bitor[T: SomeInteger](x, y: T; z: varargs[T]): T
entries.description: Computes the bitwise or of all arguments collectively.

entries.name: bitxor
entries.code: macro bitxor[T: SomeInteger](x, y: T; z: varargs[T]): T
entries.description: Computes the bitwise xor of all arguments collectively.

entries.name: BitsRange
entries.code: BitsRange[T] = range[0 .. sizeof(T) * 8 - 1]
entries.description: A range with all bit positions for type T.

entries.name: bitsliced
entries.code: func bitsliced[T: SomeInteger](v: T; slice: Slice[int]): T {.inline.}
entries.description: Returns an extracted (and shifted) slice of bits from v.

entries.name: bitslice
entries.code: proc bitslice[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; slice: Slice[int]) {.inline.}
entries.description: Mutates v into an extracted (and shifted) slice of bits from v.

entries.name: toMask
entries.code: func toMask[T: SomeInteger](slice: Slice[int]): T {.inline.}
entries.description: Creates a bitmask based on a slice of bits.

entries.name: masked
entries.code: proc masked[T: SomeInteger](v, mask: T): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with only the 1 bits from mask matching those of v set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation.

entries.name: masked
entries.code: func masked[T: SomeInteger](v: T; slice: Slice[int]): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with only the 1 bits in the range of slice matching those of v set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation.

entries.name: mask
entries.code: proc mask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; mask: T) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with only the 1 bits from mask matching those of v set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation.

entries.name: mask
entries.code: proc mask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; slice: Slice[int]) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with only the 1 bits in the range of slice matching those of v set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation.

entries.name: setMasked
entries.code: func setMasked[T: SomeInteger](v, mask: T): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with all the 1 bits from mask set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitor operation.

entries.name: setMasked
entries.code: func setMasked[T: SomeInteger](v: T; slice: Slice[int]): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with all the 1 bits in the range of slice set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitor operation.

entries.name: setMask
entries.code: proc setMask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; mask: T) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with all the 1 bits from mask set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitor operation.

entries.name: setMask
entries.code: proc setMask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; slice: Slice[int]) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with all the 1 bits in the range of slice set to 1.

Effectively maps to a bitor operation.

entries.name: clearMasked
entries.code: func clearMasked[T: SomeInteger](v, mask: T): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with all the 1 bits from mask set to 0.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation with an inverted mask.

entries.name: clearMasked
entries.code: func clearMasked[T: SomeInteger](v: T; slice: Slice[int]): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with all the 1 bits in the range of slice set to 0.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation with an inverted mask.

entries.name: clearMask
entries.code: proc clearMask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; mask: T) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with all the 1 bits from mask set to 0.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation with an inverted mask.

entries.name: clearMask
entries.code: proc clearMask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; slice: Slice[int]) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with all the 1 bits in the range of slice set to 0.

Effectively maps to a bitand operation with an inverted mask.

entries.name: flipMasked
entries.code: func flipMasked[T: SomeInteger](v, mask: T): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with all the 1 bits from mask flipped.

Effectively maps to a bitxor operation.

entries.name: flipMasked
entries.code: func flipMasked[T: SomeInteger](v: T; slice: Slice[int]): T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns v, with all the 1 bits in the range of slice flipped.

Effectively maps to a bitxor operation.

entries.name: flipMask
entries.code: proc flipMask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; mask: T) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with all the 1 bits from mask flipped.

Effectively maps to a bitxor operation.

entries.name: flipMask
entries.code: proc flipMask[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; slice: Slice[int]) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Mutates v, with all the 1 bits in the range of slice flipped.

Effectively maps to a bitxor operation.

entries.name: setBit
entries.code: proc setBit[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; bit: BitsRange[T]) {.inline.}
entries.description: Mutates v, with the bit at position bit set to 1.

entries.name: clearBit
entries.code: proc clearBit[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; bit: BitsRange[T]) {.inline.}
entries.description: Mutates v, with the bit at position bit set to 0.

entries.name: flipBit
entries.code: proc flipBit[T: SomeInteger](v: var T; bit: BitsRange[T]) {.inline.}
entries.description: Mutates v, with the bit at position bit flipped.

entries.name: setBits
entries.code: macro setBits(v: typed; bits: varargs[typed]): untyped
entries.description: Mutates v, with the bits at positions bits set to 1.

entries.name: clearBits
entries.code: macro clearBits(v: typed; bits: varargs[typed]): untyped
entries.description: Mutates v, with the bits at positions bits set to 0.

entries.name: flipBits
entries.code: macro flipBits(v: typed; bits: varargs[typed]): untyped
entries.description: Mutates v, with the bits at positions bits set to 0.

entries.name: testBit
entries.code: proc testBit[T: SomeInteger](v: T; bit: BitsRange[T]): bool {.inline.}
entries.description: Returns true if the bit in v at positions bit is set to 1.

entries.name: countSetBits
entries.code: func countSetBits(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Counts the set bits in an integer (also called Hamming weight).

entries.name: popcount
entries.code: func popcount(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Alias for countSetBits (Hamming weight).

entries.name: parityBits
entries.code: func parityBits(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Calculate the bit parity in an integer. If the number of 1-bits is odd, the parity is 1, otherwise 0.

entries.name: firstSetBit
entries.code: func firstSetBit(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Returns the 1-based index of the least significant set bit of x. If x is zero, when noUndefinedBitOpts is set, the result is 0, otherwise the result is undefined.

entries.name: fastLog2
entries.code: func fastLog2(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Quickly find the log base 2 of an integer. If x is zero, when noUndefinedBitOpts is set, the result is -1, otherwise the result is undefined.

entries.name: countLeadingZeroBits
entries.code: func countLeadingZeroBits(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns the number of leading zero bits in an integer. If x is zero, when noUndefinedBitOpts is set, the result is 0, otherwise the result is undefined.

See also:

  • countTrailingZeroBits proc

entries.name: countTrailingZeroBits
entries.code: func countTrailingZeroBits(x: SomeInteger): int {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns the number of trailing zeros in an integer. If x is zero, when noUndefinedBitOpts is set, the result is 0, otherwise the result is undefined.

See also:

  • countLeadingZeroBits proc

entries.name: rotateLeftBits
entries.description: Left-rotate bits in a value.

entries.name: rotateRightBits
entries.code:
func rotateRightBits[T: SomeUnsignedInt](value: T;
    shift: range[0 .. (sizeof(T) * 8)]): T {.inline.}
entries.description: Right-rotate bits in a value.

entries.name: reverseBits
entries.code: func reverseBits[T: SomeUnsignedInt](x: T): T
entries.description: Return the bit reversal of x.
